checks.a_analysis: The output JSON consists entirely of text present in the source PDF. All headings, paragraphs, list items, and table cell contents are derived directly from the source. The title in metadata is generated for file identification but does not represent fabricated document content. No hallucinated content was found.

The source document does not contain any contact information such as phone numbers, emails, or physical addresses. The output correctly reflects this absence and does not introduce any fabricated contact details.

All three pages of the source PDF are represented in the output JSON. Page 1's introductory sections (Purpose, Goals, Objectives, etc.) are present. The tables spanning pages 2 and 3, covering tasks 2.1 through 4.10, are fully converted. No whole sections or pages are missing.

There are two tables in the document. The table on page 2 contains 8 data rows (2.1 to 4.3) plus a header. The table on page 3 contains 7 data rows (4.4 to 4.10) plus a header. The JSON output accurately reflects the total number of rows and columns for these tables, preserving all task entries.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: A thorough comparison of the text content within each section reveals that no substantial text has been omitted. Paragraphs and list items are complete. Within the tables, lengthy descriptions (e.g., tasks 3.2 and 4.1) are preserved in their entirety. Repetitive page headers and page numbers are excluded from the body content elements, which is acceptable behavior.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The wording in the output JSON matches the source PDF exactly in terms of information. Numbers, dates, and names in the tables (e.g., '6/2020', 'John Bridgers', 'Anna Verrill') are transcribed accurately. Formatting changes, such as converting list styles and table headers into structured JSON fields, are correct and maintain semantic fidelity.

checks.a_analysis: The content in the output follows the source document without repeating blocks of text incorrectly. While the page headers ('Work Plan', 'Statewide Mapping Advisory Committee') are repeated at the beginning of each major JSON section, this is a reflection of the source PDF's design where these headers appear on every page, and thus falls under the 'Acceptable Changes' criteria.

The heading hierarchy is logical and consistent. H1 is used for the primary title, H2 for major sections (Goals, Objectives, etc.), and H3 for sub-headings like the task table title. There are no skips in hierarchy (e.g., H1 to H3).

The table spanning pages 2 and 3 of the source is rendered as two separate tables in the output, which is appropriate for a sectioned extraction. Both tables correctly identify the 5 columns and headers. Column spans and row contents match the visual structure of the PDF, including long text blocks like those in the status column of task 3.2.

The output preserves the reading order of the source document, starting with the introductory purpose, then the goals/objectives/responsibilities, and finally the task tables in the correct sequence (2.1 through 4.10).

The lists on page 1 of the source (Goals, Objectives, Expected Benefits, and Ongoing Responsibilities) are ordered lists (lettered A-C or numbered 1-4). The output converts these to unordered lists and strips the leading identifiers (e.g., 'A.', '1.') from the text content. This loses the structural numbering that allows readers to link specific benefits and tasks back to the objective numbers.
checks.b_evidence_source: 1. Communicate information on SMAC work plan activities related to policy and procedures...
checks.b_evidence_output: Communicate information on SMAC work plan activities related to policy and procedures...

checks.a_analysis: The source document contains no interactive or visible hyperlink URLs. The output correctly reflects this by not including any 'link' type elements.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the visual organization of the source PDF. The hierarchical structure of headers (Goals, Objectives, Expected Benefits, Ongoing Responsibilities) is preserved. The content from the three PDF pages is logically flowed into a single HTML document. The addition of a Table of Contents and the responsive nature of the HTML layout are expected behaviors for this type of conversion.

The tables containing the 'Major Tasks Numbered by Objective' from pages 2 and 3 of the PDF are rendered correctly in the HTML. They retain their columnar structure, headers are clearly defined, and borders are visible to differentiate rows and cells. The content remains easily readable and correctly aligned.
The source PDF contains no meaningful images, charts, or logos. It is a text-and-table document. Therefore, no images are missing in the HTML output.

There is no visual evidence of duplicated body content. While headers appear in both the Table of Contents and as headings within the body text, this is a standard and expected accessibility feature and does not constitute content duplication.
